summary: Kenneth discusses foundational investment advice for an 18-year-old's first paycheck, recommending Roth IRA accounts, S&P 500 index funds, and dividend ETFs as long-term wealth building vehicles. The video emphasizes the power of compound growth over 30-40 years and the importance of emergency savings, but does not address options trading, premium selling, or active portfolio management relevant to Brian's Premium Desk strategy.
takeaways: Save $250/month invested in S&P 500 index fund at 10.5% historical return = ~$1.9M in 40 years
Maintain 6-8 month emergency fund before investing
56% of Americans cannot cover $1,000 emergency expense
Avoid get-rich-quick schemes that lead to losses
Open Roth IRA as tax-advantaged account for long-term growth
title: How I’d invest if I were 18 years old
